error_signature: Returned type uuid does not match expected type bigint in column 1
title: UUID vs BigInt type mismatch in Supabase function
symptom: When executing a function in Supabase, an error occurs stating 'Returned type uuid does not match expected type bigint in column 1'. This prevents successful execution of the function.
applies_when: Using Supabase with a function that returns a UUID
The expected return type is BigInt
not_this_if: Function is correctly defined with matching types
No UUIDs are involved in the function's return
root_cause: The error arises when the function's return type does not match the actual data type of the column in the database. In this case, the function was expecting a BigInt but was receiving a UUID instead, leading to a type mismatch.
fix_steps: Ensure all relevant tables use the same ID type (e.g., change UUID to BigInt).
Delete any existing functions that reference the old ID type.
Create or replace the function with the correct return type matching the table's ID type.
agent_instruction: Unify the ID types in your tables to BigInt and recreate the necessary functions.
